world.title: New Zealand reports possible N. Korea sanctions breaches at sea

world.description: The head of the national rescue agency says efforts continue to remove people trapped in wreckage.

world.description: The world is spending more money on weapons than ever before, reaching nearly $4 trillion in 2025.

world.title: Women-only carriage bore the brunt of Jakarta train crash
world.description: A Jakarta train collision saw a women-only carriage suffer severe damage, resulting in casualties and injuries in one of Indonesia's worst rail accidents. Read more at straitstimes.com.

world.title: Did Kash Patel’s Hindu identity keep him off Trump dinner shooter’s hit list?
world.description: Investigators are probing why Kash Patel was excluded from the WHCD gunman’s hit list, examining religion, strategy and political motives behind the manifesto.

world.title: Somalia Drought Crisis Displaces Over 500,000 Amid Hunger Surge
world.description: Across Somalia, a worsening hunger crisis is unfolding as prolonged drought continues to devastate communities, destroy livelihoods, and force large-scale displacement. The failed September Deyr rains have further deepened the climate emergency, leading to repeated harvest failures and widespread loss of livestock, which many families depend on for survival. business.title: Johari open to EVs for ministers, but says charging network must come first
business.description: KUALA LUMPUR: Cabinet minister Datuk Seri Johari Abdul Ghani says he is open to Cabinet ministers using electric vehicles (EVs) as part of a cost-cutting measure amid the ongoing global oil crisis

business.title: PSU bank stocks fall up to 3%. How RBI’s ECL directions can impact lenders
business.description: PSU bank stocks fell up to 3% on Tuesday after RBI confirmed the rollout of the Expected Credit Loss (ECL) framework from April 2027, triggering concerns over higher provisioning and capital impact. Brokerages warn of a potential 5–10% hit to net worth, though phased implementation may soften the immediate blow.

business.title: Woman questions Prudential's head of life claims in lawsuit seeking brain surgery payout as insurer responds
business.description: Ms Cai Yunhong questioned Prudential's head of life claims on the way her policy documents were drafted, alleging that they were "convoluted" and "full of ambiguities".

business.title: Stranded in mountains, Vietnamese student survives for 37 hours on Choco Pies
business.description: A college student stranded in the mountains of Vietnam reportedly survived for 37 hours on just Orion Choco Pies. The Korean snack, already beloved...

business.title: GWM eyes V8 return to Australia with Tank 700 SUV
business.description: Great Wall Motors, is eyeing the addition of V8 power in Australia, with a new flagship SUV leading the charge.

business.title: First LNG Shipment Crosses Strait Of Hormuz Since West Asia War Began
business.description: The vessel had stopped transmitting its signal around March 31, only to reappear near Indian waters this week.

business.title: Hong Kong home prices rise higher in March as rents peak for a fifth month straight
business.description: Prices of the city’s residential property rose by 1.4 per cent, showing strong resilience despite economic turbulence caused by the Iran war.

business.title: 'Backup buffer': Government buys 90 million more litres of diesel
business.description: The fuel will be delivered to Marsden Point in Northland and stored in a refurbished tank at Channel Infrastructure's site as early as late June.

science.title: How to catch the Eta Aquarid meteor shower this May
science.description: The Eta Aquarid meteor shower peaks in early May over Australia. Here’s how to see it, including best viewing times and conditions.

science.title: See Halley's Comet (Well, Sort of ...)
science.description: Week of May 3-9, 2026 If you're old enough, do you remember seeing Halley's Comet — the most famous of all celestial nomads — when it swung past our neck of the cosmic woods in 1986? I did, and I wasn't terribly impressed. It was arguably one of the comet's worst appearances in some 2,000 years.

science.title: Two whale groups separated by seas-but not by genes, study finds
science.description: A paper in Genome Biology and Evolution discovers that the endangered Mediterranean fin whale is not completely isolated from Atlantic groups. Both Atlantic and Mediterranean populations have declined for the past 200,000 years. Considering more recent threats to the whales, this finding has important implications for conservation, particularly considering increasing anthropogenic pressures.

science.title: Two whale groups separated by seas-but not by genes, study finds
science.description: A new paper in Genome Biology and Evolution discovers that the endangered Mediterranean fin whale is not completely isolated from Atlantic groups. Both Atlantic and Mediterranean populations have declined for the past 200,000 years. Considering more recent threats to the whales, this finding has important implications for conservation, particularly considering increasing anthropogenic pressures.

science.title: Mapping molecular markers of physical fitness
science.description: The PhenoMol computational model analyzes biomarkers of cellular activity to help predict an individual’s physical fitness level. The model could advance future studies to improve fitness training and speed injury or disease recovery.

ai.title: ‘It guessed instead of verifying’: How an AI coding agent deleted company’s entire database
ai.description: PocketOS founder Jer Crane reveals how a Claude Opus 4.6 AI agent deleted his production database, exposing hidden risks in AI driven development workflows

ai.title: Foundation stone laid for Google Cloud India AI Hub in Visakhapatnam under Adani-Google-Airtel data centre project
ai.description: Andhra Pradesh Chief Minister N Chandrababu Naidu on Tuesday laid the foundation stone of the Google Cloud India AI Hub in Visakhapatnam, marking a major step in building large-scale digital and AI infrastructure in the state.

ai.title: Ashwini Vaishnaw arrives in Visakhapatnam for Google data centre foundation ceremony
ai.description: Union Minister Ashwini Vaishnaw on Tuesday arrived in Visakhapatnam to participate in the foundation stone laying ceremony of the Google AI Data Centre at Tarluvada in Anandapuram mandal.

ai.title: AI imagines Deepika Padukone in Russian, Mongolian, Chinese, more looks: Internet says ‘she looks cute as South Korean’
ai.description: AI artist Mr Hellrocker reimagined Deepika Padukone in various global looks, showcasing her in traditional attire from Brazil, Nigeria, Mongolia, and more. | Fashion Trends

ai.title: AI data centres’ gas gamble could cost the planet more than entire countries
ai.description: As AI fuels an unprecedented data centre boom, tech giants are turning to gas-powered energy for speed and control. But soaring costs and alarming emissions data reveal a deeper problem: the infrastructure built to power the future could significantly worsen the climate crisis and reshape global energy priorities.

ai.title: Andhra Pradesh Chief Minister Lays Foundation For $15 Billion Google AI Data Centre
ai.description: Andhra Pradesh Chief Minister N Chandrababu Naidu lays foundation for $15B Google AI data centre near Visakhapatnam.

ai.title: Google AI data centre Vizag: CM Naidu kickstarts $15 billion push to build AI and cloud hub
ai.description: Google AI data centre Vizag: Andhra Pradesh Chief Minister N. Chandrababu Naidu inaugurated the Google Cloud India AI Hub in Visakhapatnam. This marks a major development in digital and artificial intelligence infrastructure for the state. The project, part of a larger data centre initiative, signifies substantial foreign investment.

ai.title: Microsoft, OpenAI announce amended deal to simplify partnership while ending exclusivity
ai.description: Microsoft and OpenAI have announced a sweeping overhaul of their partnership, easing exclusivity clauses and allowing the ChatGPT maker to expand its presence across rival cloud platforms.
